steps.title: If leeches are about a pinky...
steps.instruction: If leeches are about a pinky fingernail size, homogenize leech tissues as section 3. Add 400 µL 1x PBS and 100 µL ATL-DX buffer in a Thermo Scientific Screw Cap 1.5 mL Micro tube containing 0.1 mm beads from the step 3 in "Before Start" section.

steps.title: Clean forceps with 70% ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Clean forceps with 70% ethanol and Kimwipes before use and between samples.

steps.title: Use ice-cold 1x PBS to wash...
steps.instruction: Use ice-cold 1x PBS to wash leech three times sequentially in petri dishes on ice to remove external contaminants.

steps.title: Use a pair of scissors to...
steps.instruction: Use a pair of scissors to cut across the leech in the middle.

steps.title: Drip the blood meal into the...
steps.instruction: Drip the blood meal into the petri dish as much as possible.

steps.title: Place the leech back into the...
steps.instruction: Place the leech back into the petri dish, use a scalpel to cut open the entire leech longitudinally.

steps.title: Open the leech with forceps, use...
steps.instruction: Open the leech with forceps, use a sterile cotton swab to wipe the opened inner organs thoroughly.

steps.title: Put the cotton tip of the swab in a 1
steps.instruction: Put the cotton tip of the swab in a 1.5 mL tube prepared in step 3, cut off the plastic handle from the cotton tip.

steps.title: Set the controls for Speed 10 and Time 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Set the controls for Speed 10 and Time 00:03:00 . Press Start.

steps.title: After the run, remove the tubes...
steps.instruction: After the run, remove the tubes from the instrument and visually inspect the samples. If homogenization is incomplete, repeat the homogenization at speed 10 and Time 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the suspension at 100 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the suspension at 100 x g, , 1 minute to pellet debris.

steps.title: Include a positive control for each batch of samples
steps.instruction: Include a positive control for each batch of samples: transfer 37.5 µL ZymoBIOMICS Microbial Community Standard Material and 100 µL EBV, and 100 µL DENV-1 standard into a Screw Cap 1.5 mL MicroTube containing 0.1 mm beating beads. Add 162.5 µL 1x PBS and 100 µL ATL-DX buffer.

steps.title: Include a negative control for each batch of samples
steps.instruction: Include a negative control for each batch of samples: add 400 µL sterile 1xPBS and 100 µL ATL-DX Buffer to a Screw Cap 1.5 mL MicroTube containing 0.1 mm beating beads.

steps.title: Load the tubes with blood meal...
steps.instruction: Load the tubes with blood meal and/or cotton tip or leech tissue lysate from step 13 and/or 14 and/or 27 into the fully cooled Bullet Blender (including samples and controls). Refill the dry ice compartment if necessary.

steps.title: Set the speed at 12 and time at 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Set the speed at 12 and time at 00:05:00 . Press Start.

steps.title: Let the samples settle for 00:01:00...
steps.instruction: Let the samples settle for 00:01:00 and then repeat step 31.

steps.title: Confirm 96 deep-well magnetic heads and...
steps.instruction: Confirm 96 deep-well magnetic heads and 96 well deep-well heat blocks are being used.

steps.title: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash has been...
steps.instruction: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash has been downloaded and loaded onto the KingFisher Flex instrument.

steps.title: Set up the Wash, Elution, and...
steps.instruction: Set up the Wash, Elution, and Tip Comb Plates outside the instrument according to the following table: NOTE : DO NOT use the elution buffer provided by the kit for TNA elution. The ingredients in the elution buffer inhibit the downstream DNA sequencing efficiency. %s %s Plate ID Plate position Plate type Reagent Volume per well Tip comb 7 Place a 96 Deep-well Tip comb in a deep-well plate Elution 6 Deep-Well Nuclease-free water 75 µL Wash 4 5 Deep-Well 100 % ethanol 750 µL Wash 3 4 Deep-Well 80% ethanol 750 µL Wash 2 3 Deep-Well Buffer AW2 700 µL Wash 1 2 Deep-Well Buffer AW1 700 µL Sample 1 Sample Lysate Lysate and lysis buffer 985 µL

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K into wells (based on number of samples) of a new deep-well plate.

steps.title: Transfer 270 µL supernatant of step...
steps.instruction: Transfer 270 µL supernatant of step 36 without any particle carryover to the wells of the Deep-well plate containing proteinase K. This plate becomes the Sample Plate.

steps.title: Add 135 µL Buffer VXL, 540...
steps.instruction: Add 135 µL Buffer VXL, 540 µL Buffer ACB, and 25 µL MagAttract Suspension G to each sample in the sample plate. For multiple samples, make a master mix with 10% overage. Invert slowly to mix the master mix, avoid foaming (can be mixed on Hula mixer for 2 min). Add 700 µL mixture to each sample.

steps.title: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash on the instrument
steps.instruction: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash on the instrument.

steps.title: Start the run, then load the...
steps.instruction: Start the run, then load the prepared plates into position when prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: After the running protocol is completed...
steps.instruction: After the running protocol is completed (~35 minutes), immediately remove the elution plate from the instrument and cover the plate or transfer the eluate to the final tube or plate of choice for final storage.

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.6 mL microcentrifuge tube, use 1 µL total nucleic acid for DNA and RNA concentration measurement using Qubit 4 Fluorometer following manufacturer instructions.

steps.title: Proceed with sample testing following the...
steps.instruction: Proceed with sample testing following the REDI-NET SOP L-4 Leech Testing or store at -20 °C for less than 2 weeks. For long-term storage the sample needs to be stored at -80 °C following the REDI-NET SOP L-3 Leech Storage.

steps.title: Confirm 12-tip magnetic head and 12...
steps.instruction: Confirm 12-tip magnetic head and 12 well deep-well heat blocks are being used.

steps.title: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash has been...
steps.instruction: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash has been downloaded and loaded onto the KingFisher Duo Prime instrument.

steps.title: Set up the Sample Plate according to the table below
steps.instruction: Set up the Sample Plate according to the table below: %s %s Row ID Plate Row Reagent Volume per well Sample row A Lysate and lysis buffer 985 µL Wash 1 B Buffer AW1 700 µL Wash 2 C Buffer AW2 700 µL Wash 3 D 80 % ethanol 750 µL Wash 4 E 100 % ethanol 750 µL Tip Comb F Tip comb 700 µL G Empty H

steps.title: Set up the Elution Strip according to the table below
steps.instruction: Set up the Elution Strip according to the table below: Note: DO NOT use the elution buffer provided by the kit for TNA elution. The ingredients in the elution buffer inhibit the downstream DNA sequencing efficiency. %s %s Row ID Plate Row Reagent Volume per well Elution A Nuclease-free water 75 µL

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K into wells (based on number of samples) of a sample row.

steps.title: Transfer 270 µL supernatant without any...
steps.instruction: Transfer 270 µL supernatant without any particle carryover to the wells of the sample row containing proteinase K. This plate becomes the Sample Plate.

steps.title: Add 135 µL Buffer VXL, 540...
steps.instruction: Add 135 µL Buffer VXL, 540 µL Buffer ACB, and 20 µL MagAttract Suspension G to each sample in the sample row. For multiple samples, make a master mix with 10% overage. Invert slowly to mix the master mix, avoid foaming. Add 695 µL mixture to each sample.

steps.title: Select program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash on the instrument
steps.instruction: Select program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash on the instrument.

steps.title: Start the run, then load the...
steps.instruction: Start the run, then load the prepared plate/strip into position when prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: After the protocol is completed (~35...
steps.instruction: After the protocol is completed (~35 minutes), immediately remove the elution strip from the instrument and transfer the eluate to the final tube or plate of choice for final storage.

steps.title: Use 1 µL total nucleic acid...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µL total nucleic acid for DNA and RNA concentration measurement using Qubit 4 Fluorometer. Kits needed : Qubit 1X dsDNA HS Assay Kit and Qubit RNA HS Assay Kit (see Appendix 4).

steps.title: Proceed with sample testing following the...
steps.instruction: Proceed with sample testing following the REDI-NET SOP L-4 Leech Testing or store at -20 °C for less than 2 weeks. For long-term storage the sample needs to be stored at -80 °C following the REDI-NET SOP L-3 Leech Storage.

steps.title: Leeches from each vial (either freshly...
steps.instruction: Leeches from each vial (either freshly from the field/animals or stored at -80 °C ) should be morphologically examined to identify its species. Only parasitic Euhirudinea species will be kept and other species will be discarded.

steps.title: Leeches can be stored at -20...
steps.instruction: Leeches can be stored at -20 °C for up to 2 weeks and at -80 °C for up to 1 month. Long term storage at 4 °C is not recommended.

steps.title: Place the rinsed leech on Kimwipes...
steps.instruction: Place the rinsed leech on Kimwipes to absorb the ethanol residuals. Place the leech in a petri dish on ice.

steps.title: Rinse leech with 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Rinse leech with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Transfer the blood meal to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the blood meal to a 1.5 mL tube by pipetting.

steps.title: For processing bloodmeal, add 50 µL...
steps.instruction: For processing bloodmeal, add 50 µL bloodmeal to a tube prepared in step 3. For those leeches without blood meal, only prepare the cotton swab. See Appendix 1 for the photos of blood meal and cotton swab collected from leeches.

steps.title: The cotton swabs and blood meal...
steps.instruction: The cotton swabs and blood meal in lysis buffer can be stored at -20 °C for a few days before processing.

steps.title: Clean forceps with 70% ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Clean forceps with 70% ethanol and Kimwipes before use and between samples.

steps.title: Keep an empty Petri dish on ice
steps.instruction: Keep an empty Petri dish on ice. Cut the leech from head to tail, and trim the leech tissue into small pieces around 3 mm x 3 mm in the Petri dish.

steps.title: Add one piece of 3 mm...
steps.instruction: Add one piece of 3 mm x 3 mm leech tissue into a navy RINO tube prepared in step 17.

steps.title: Load the RINO tubes with leech...
steps.instruction: Load the RINO tubes with leech tissue into the Bullet Blender. Add more dry ice into the cooling compartment of the Bullet Blender, if necessary.

steps.title: Repeat step 22
steps.instruction: Repeat step 22.

steps.title: Add 80 µL ATL-DTX buffer into the tube
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL ATL-DTX buffer into the tube.

steps.title: Use ice-cold1x PBS to wash leech...
steps.instruction: Use ice-cold1x PBS to wash leech three times sequentially in Petri dishes on ice to remove external contaminants.

steps.title: Label navy RINO RNA lysis tubes...
steps.instruction: Label navy RINO RNA lysis tubes on the cap, and add 60 µL cold, sterile 1x PBS into each navy RINO tube (avoid labeling on the side of the tubes due to potential damage during the beads beating process)

steps.title: Without disturbing the tubes, carefully transfer...
steps.instruction: Without disturbing the tubes, carefully transfer the top 320 µL supernatant into the 1.5 mL tubes of "Before Start" section step 3.

steps.title: After beating, centrifuge the tubes with...
steps.instruction: After beating, centrifuge the tubes with lysate for 12000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes STOPPING POINT : lysed samples can be stored at 4 °C Overnight .
